intro: Caprifolium × coerulescens, also known as blue hawthorn, is a hybrid shrub or small tree that belongs to the Caprifoliaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiovascular benefits, which are attributed to its ability to help lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The bioactive constituents of blue hawthorn include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Blue hawthorn is commonly prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to promote cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
